goal: Land the Graphiti (G3 Knowledge) and CodeGraphContext (G4 Architecture) external MCP plugin manifests with real-subprocess `tools/list` health-check integration tests, mirroring the WO-0069 template; F08 also establishes the new `architecture.*` capability namespace.
plan_summary: Reuse the WO-0069 ast-grep+probe / codebase-memory+mem0 plugin-manifest landing template verbatim for two parallel external plugins. F10 adds plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh + a third test inside the existing mod g3_plugin_manifests block in tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs (extending, not duplicating). F08 adds plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-codegraphcontext-mcp.sh + a NEW test file tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs with a NEW mod g4_plugin_manifests block (architecture.* is a fresh namespace; group-specific opt-out env UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E mirrors UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E). Both manifests pin to immutable npm/pypi release tags (no `main`/`latest`/etc.). Both verify scripts mirror P3-W9-F05/F06 exactly: cargo test summary regex + tool-level smoke gated on UCIL_SKIP_<group>_PLUGIN_E2E and (where API keys apply) on the API-key env presence per WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 (pre-emptive API-key-gate carve-out). Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract per the 5-WO mature pattern (WO-0066/0067/0068/0069/0070).
scope_in: 1. Identify upstream pins at WO-execution time and document the source URL + immutable release tag in the manifest's leading comment block. Acceptable forms: npm tag (e.g., `npx -y <pkg>@<ver>`) OR pypi tag (e.g., `uvx <pkg>@<ver>`). Forbidden: `main`, `latest`, `master`, `head`, `develop`, `dev`, `nightly`, OR git-branch refs. Cite the upstream's official MCP-server repo (Graphiti: getzep/graphiti or upstream-published `graphiti-core[mcp]` / `graphiti-mcp` pypi package; CodeGraphContext: Shashankss1205/CodeGraphContext upstream — verify which package name is canonically published before pinning).

2. Create plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml mirroring the structure of plugins/knowledge/mem0/plugin.toml (F06): leading multi-line comment with `pinned <DATE>:` block (source URL, package URL, pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools list with count, transport=stdio, API keys notes); `[plugin]` section (name='graphiti', version=<pinned>, description citing master-plan §4.3 line 313 + §5.3 G3 source designation + bi-temporal hallmark); `[capabilities]` section with provides=['knowledge.temporal_query', 'knowledge.fact_validity_lookup'] (or analogous knowledge.* names — the namespace is established and these new capabilities extend it cleanly); languages=[] (the temporal knowledge graph is text-keyed agent observations, not code-language-keyed, mirroring mem0); `[transport]` section with type='stdio', command/args matching the upstream MCP launcher; `[lifecycle]` section mirroring F05/F06 exactly (hot_cold=true, idle_timeout_minutes=10).

3. Create plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml mirroring the structure of F05's codebase-memory/plugin.toml: leading multi-line comment with `pinned <DATE>:` block (source URL, package URL, pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools list with the master-plan §4.4 line 326 invariant '14 languages', transport=stdio, API keys notes — confirm whether CodeGraphContext requires any API keys or runs entirely local); `[plugin]` section (name='codegraphcontext', version=<pinned>, description citing master-plan §4.4 line 326 + §5.4 G4 source designation + 'dependency graph + blast radius analysis. MIT license.'); `[capabilities]` section with provides=['architecture.dependency_graph', 'architecture.blast_radius'] (NEW `architecture.*` capability namespace prefix — establish parallel to `search.*`/`navigate.*`/`knowledge.*`); languages=[<the languages CodeGraphContext supports per upstream — at minimum rust+typescript+python+go+java per the master-plan claim of 14 languages, but executor verifies the actual set from upstream]); `[transport]` section type='stdio'; `[lifecycle]` section mirroring F05/F06 exactly.

4. Establish the `architecture.*` capability namespace explicitly in the codegraphcontext manifest's leading comment block (section equivalent to mem0's 'Establishing the knowledge.* capability namespace' paragraph): cite §4.4 G4 group, parallel to existing `search.*` (probe), `navigate.*` (scip), `knowledge.*` (codebase-memory/mem0), and indicate subsequent G4 plugins (gitnexus, dep-cruiser, axon, deptry, nx, bazel — see master-plan §14.1 line 1677) follow this convention.

5. Create scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh mirroring scripts/devtools/install-mem0-mcp.sh (or install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh if the upstream uses npx) verbatim in shape: `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_*_VERSION=` constant, OK-when-binary-on-PATH branch, OK-when-uvx/npx-on-PATH branch, MISSING branch with the exact pinned install command (`uv tool install ...==<ver>` or `npm install -g ...@<ver>`). Always `exit 0` (informational scaffolding).

6. Create scripts/devtools/install-codegraphcontext-mcp.sh mirroring scripts/devtools/install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh (or install-mem0-mcp.sh) verbatim in shape. Same exit-0 informational-scaffolding contract.

7. Extend crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs by ADDING a third test `graphiti_manifest_health_check` INSIDE the existing `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (between `mem0_manifest_health_check` and the closing `}`). Mirror the existing two tests' shape exactly: `#[tokio::test] async fn`, `if skip_via_env() { return; }`, load manifest, manifest sanity asserts (name='graphiti', transport.kind='stdio', !provides.is_empty()), `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(&manifest, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS)`, assert health.name='graphiti', health.status=Ok, !health.tools.is_empty(), and a load-bearing assertion that one specific advertised tool name is present (executor selects the canonical Graphiti tool — likely 'add_episode' / 'search_facts' / 'add_observation' or whichever name the upstream MCP server canonically publishes — and pins on it; rationale comment cites that pinning on the count would force updates on every benign upstream tool addition while pinning on a specific name surfaces upstream tool-surface drift loudly, mirroring the mem0 `add_memory` / codebase-memory `search_graph` rationale).

8. Create crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs as a NEW test file mirroring tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs in shape: leading `//!` rustdoc explaining the suite's role (G4 architecture plugin manifests, real subprocess + real JSON-RPC, mocking forbidden, rationale for separate-file-per-group with the `UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E` opt-out cited), `mod g4_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at module root, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS=90_000, repo_root() helper, `skip_via_env()` honouring `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` AND `UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E`, single test `codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` of the same shape as the G3 tests. Cite WO-0069 lessons §executor #2 ('write a SEPARATE integration test file per phase/group') in the rustdoc.

9. Both new tests live INSIDE their respective `mod <name>_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at module root (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, carried per WO-0068 lessons §planner 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement' — for nextest/cargo-test the path becomes `<file>::<module>::<test>` which substring-matches the cargo-test selector cleanly). Confirm `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` and `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` resolve uniquely.

10. Create scripts/verify/P3-W9-F08.sh by copying scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh verbatim and adapting: change PINNED_NPM_VERSION/PINNED_PYPI_VERSION + PINNED_PKG_SPEC + the cargo-test selector to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check`. Tool-level smoke (Step 2 in F05) MUST gate on `UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E` (NOT KNOWLEDGE) and exercise a representative dependency-graph or blast-radius CLI invocation against tests/fixtures/rust-project (executor selects whichever CodeGraphContext CLI surface is most representative of master-plan §4.4 'dependency graph, blast radius analysis' — e.g., index then query for blast-radius of a specific function in tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs). The script remains read-only against tests/fixtures/rust-project (forbidden_paths). Print `[OK] P3-W9-F08` on green; `[FAIL] P3-W9-F08: <reason>` on red. Always `exit 0` on green / `exit 1` on red.

11. Replace scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh (currently TODO-stub) by copying scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh verbatim and adapting: change pinned-version constants + the cargo-test selector to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check`. Tool-level smoke MUST gate on `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E` (existing G3 opt-out) AND on the API-key env presence if Graphiti requires one (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY for Graphiti's LLM-backed extraction — executor verifies upstream contract). Print `[OK] P3-W9-F10` on green; `[FAIL] P3-W9-F10: <reason>` on red.

12. Pre-baked mutation contract M1 (graphiti transport.command poison): swap `command = "<launcher>"` → `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"` in plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml; cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` must FAIL with `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` (or analogous spawn-error chain on the platform); restore via `git checkout -- plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml`; confirm md5sum matches the pre-mutation snapshot.

13. Pre-baked mutation contract M2 (codegraphcontext transport.command poison): swap `command = "<launcher>"` → `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M2__"` in plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml; cargo test selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` must FAIL with the same spawn-error chain; restore + md5 confirm.

14. Pre-baked mutation contract M3 (expected-tool-name regression on EITHER target): in tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs (graphiti test) OR tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs (codegraphcontext test), change the `t == "<canonical_tool>"` assertion to a non-existent tool name (e.g., `t == "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M3"`); the test MUST FAIL with the structured panic carrying the full advertised-tool list (the panic body itself doubles as a diagnostic dump of the live tool surface at the pinned version). Restore + md5 confirm.

15. Pre-flight word-ban grep MUST cover ONLY production-side files (manifests, install scripts, verify scripts) and EXEMPT integration-test files under `#[cfg(test)]`/`tests/`. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh scripts/devtools/install-codegraphcontext-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W9-F0[81]0].sh` must return empty before first push. The integration-test file's rustdoc may mention 'Mocking ... is forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration (per WO-0048 line 363 `#[cfg(test)]` carve-out, carried by WO-0069).

16. Pinned-immutable-tag policy: confirm `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml` returns empty (the floating-ref class).

17. Both `transport.command` fields MUST be the bare binary name (`"npx"`, `"uvx"`, `"docker"`, etc.), NOT an absolute path — per WO-0069 lessons §executor: the M1/M2 transport.command-poison mutation pattern relies on swapping to a `/__ucil_test_nonexistent_*__` path, AND absolute paths break across CI/dev environments.

18. Augment crates/ucil-daemon's `Cargo.toml` ONLY if a new dev-dep is required; otherwise reuse the existing `tokio = { workspace = true, features = [...] }` and `toml = { workspace = true }` deps. NO net-new workspace deps expected (the WO-0069 template required none beyond what was already present). If a dev-dep is required, declare via `<dep>.workspace = true` (mirroring WO-0068 + WO-0069 patterns) and add the dep at the workspace root with the actual version pin.

19. Two coordinated features bundled per the WO-0067/0068/0069/0070 precedent — both are external plugin manifests with shared scope_in shape, both depend on P2-W6-F01 alone, and both extend the §13 (Plugin manifest) + §14.1 (Plugins directory) contract. The capability-namespace work in F08 (architecture.*) is a load-bearing enabler for the future F09 G4 parallel architecture executor that will sit downstream.

20. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out applies to BOTH the new tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs file (single cohesive ~165-LOC commit per WO-0069 commit `bfd984f` precedent) AND the augmentation of tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs (single small commit adding ONE test inside the existing `mod` block — likely <60 LOC). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0069 commit `bfd984f` in the relevant commit bodies.

21. §15.2 tracing spans do NOT apply to plugin-manifest landings — these features add data files (TOML manifests) + integration tests + helper scripts, NOT new orchestration code. Do not introduce new `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations under this WO. Cite WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 ('pure-deterministic / data-only modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply') as precedent.

22. WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 carve-out (pre-emptive API-key-gate for plugins requiring keys): if Graphiti requires an LLM API key (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY) for `tools/list` to succeed, the verify script's tool-level smoke section MAY short-circuit with `[SKIP] P3-W9-F10: tool-level smoke skipped — <KEY> unset` + `exit 0`; cargo-test `tools/list` path remains the load-bearing assertion. Critic should not flag this as a deviation under this scope_in citation. If the upstream's `tools/list` works without an API key (mem0 precedent), this gate is unused.

23. WO-0069 lessons §planner #3 carve-out (eventual-consistency OR-disjunction): if Graphiti's tool-level smoke includes a round-trip assertion against an embedding-index or temporal-validity surface with eventual-consistency semantics, the assertion MUST be OR-disjunctive (`if 'wo-0071' not in search_text and 'wo-0071' not in retrieve_text:`) rather than AND-conjunctive. Critic should not flag this as a deviation under this scope_in citation.

24. Frozen-test multi-thread tokio flavor is NOT required for plugin-manifest tests — the test does not assert parallelism; the existing `#[tokio::test]` (single-thread default) suffices, mirroring tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs's existing two tests. WO-0070 lessons §executor 'multi-thread flavor required for parallelism SA1' applies only to fan-out orchestration tests.

25. AC25 reworded per WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' (using `git log feat ^main --merges` returning empty), NOT the WO-0070-and-prior 'feat..main = 0' form. Same spirit, more precise wording — tolerates 1-commit divergence between feat and main caused by gate-side critic-report commits between executor RFR push and verifier startup.

26. Carry-forward standing protocol (26 WOs deep, WO-0058..WO-0070): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings}` lines in phase-3.sh output per scope_out + AC standing protocol.

27. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC36/AC37 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations: 20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md, 20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md, 20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke + Serena-docker + diagnostics-bridge sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item; awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO.

28. Verifier MUST NOT set `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` OR `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E` OR `UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E` envs (per WO-0069 scope_in #5 precedent extended to this WO's new G4 opt-out).

29. RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include the M1/M2/M3 mutation contract section with: (a) named file path, (b) the exact patch (e.g., `s/command = "npx"/command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"/`), (c) the targeted cargo-test selector, (d) the expected panic line, (e) the restore command (`git checkout -- <path>`), (f) the pre-mutation md5 snapshot path (`/tmp/wo-0071-<file>-orig.md5`). Mirror the WO-0067/0068/0069/0070 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

30. Pre-flight `which npx uvx` (or whichever launchers the chosen pins use) before first push — the integration tests will spawn from PATH, and missing launchers produce false-positive `PluginError::Spawn { kind: NotFound }` which masks real M1/M2 detection. Per WO-0069 lessons §verifier #2.

31. Pre-baked md5 snapshots: take `md5sum plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0071-graphiti-orig.md5` AND `md5sum plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0071-codegraphcontext-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0071-g3tests-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0071-g4tests-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation. Confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0071-<name>-orig.md5` returning OK after each `git checkout --` restore.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT implement a `G3Source` or `G4Source` adapter for either Graphiti or CodeGraphContext (those are deferred to follow-up WOs that wire the daemon-side plugin runtime against the F07 G3 executor and a future F09 G4 executor).
2. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (WO-0044 P2-W6-F05/F06 regression guard) — keep that suite isolated per WO-0069 lessons §executor #2.
3. Do NOT modify the existing two tests inside `mod g3_plugin_manifests` (codebase_memory_manifest_health_check, mem0_manifest_health_check) — only ADD the third graphiti test.

4. Do NOT add new G3 or G4 plugin manifests beyond Graphiti and CodeGraphContext under this WO (arc-memory, cognee, conport, gitnexus, dep-cruiser, axon, deptry, nx, bazel are all future-WO scope per master-plan §14.1 line 1676-1677).

5. Do NOT wire the new manifests into any `PluginManager::initialize_all` / startup-discovery path beyond what already exists — the WO-0044 + WO-0069 manifests are loaded on-demand by the integration tests via `PluginManifest::from_path(&manifest_path)`, and the new manifests should be loadable the same way without daemon-startup changes.

6. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider or embedding-provider configuration (Graphiti's optional LLM backend is operator-scoped; UCIL only consumes the MCP `tools/list` surface, not the underlying LLM).
7. Do NOT introduce any cross-WO dependency wiring (P3-W9-F09 G4 architecture executor consumes F08; that's a downstream WO).
8. Do NOT add any new `tracing::instrument` annotations to source code (per scope_in #21).
9. Do NOT add or modify entries in `ucil-build/feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.

10. Do NOT modify any file under `tests/fixtures/**` — verify scripts are read-only against the fixture (per WO-0069 forbidden_paths). The verify scripts may invoke CLI tools that READ from the fixture but MUST NOT modify it.

12. Standing carry: do NOT touch `scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh` to fix the sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` interaction — that is a Bucket B / Bucket D harness-improvement candidate per WO-0058..WO-0070 lessons. Per scope_in #26 standing protocol.
13. Standing carry: do NOT remediate the three pre-existing effectiveness-gate flake escalations under this WO — those await a dedicated harness-improvement WO. Per scope_in #27 standing protocol.
14. Do NOT add `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, or commented-out assertions to silence any test (anti-laziness contract).
15. Do NOT use `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, or `pass`-only bodies (anti-laziness contract).

16. Standing carry: do NOT attempt to fix `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug (carries from WO-0067/0068/0069/0070; informational-only at this point — the M1/M2/M3 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer).
acceptance: 1. plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml exists, is valid TOML, and parses cleanly via `PluginManifest::from_path` to {name='graphiti', version=<pinned>, transport.kind='stdio', capabilities.provides non-empty}.

2. plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml exists, is valid TOML, and parses cleanly via `PluginManifest::from_path` to {name='codegraphcontext', version=<pinned>, transport.kind='stdio', capabilities.provides non-empty, capabilities.provides[0] starts with 'architecture.'}.
3. The `architecture.*` capability namespace is established in the codegraphcontext manifest's leading comment block.
4. scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh and scripts/devtools/install-codegraphcontext-mcp.sh both exit 0 and print the OK / MISSING branches correctly per their environment.
5. crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs's `mod g3_plugin_manifests` block contains THREE tests (codebase_memory, mem0, graphiti); the existing two tests are byte-identical to pre-WO state.
6. crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs is a NEW file containing `mod g4_plugin_manifests` block with ONE test (codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check).
7. `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` exits 0 with `1 passed; 0 failed` summary.
8. `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` exits 0 with `1 passed; 0 failed` summary.
9. scripts/verify/P3-W9-F08.sh exits 0 (with tool-level smoke gated on UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E + any API-key env per upstream contract).
10. scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh exits 0 (with tool-level smoke gated on UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E + any API-key env per upstream contract).

11. M1 mutation (graphiti transport.command poison) flips the `g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check` test from PASS to FAIL with a spawn-error chain; restoration + md5 match returns the test to PASS.

12. M2 mutation (codegraphcontext transport.command poison) flips the `g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` test from PASS to FAIL with a spawn-error chain; restoration + md5 match returns the test to PASS.
13. M3 mutation (expected-tool-name regression on either test) flips that test from PASS to FAIL with a structured panic carrying the live tool list; restoration + md5 match returns the test to PASS.

14. `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh scripts/devtools/install-codegraphcontext-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W9-F08.sh scripts/verify/P3-W9-F10.sh` returns empty.
15. `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml` returns empty.
16. `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon -- -D warnings` exits 0.
17. `cargo fmt --all -- --check` exits 0.
18. `git log feat/WO-0071-graphiti-and-codegraphcontext-plugin-manifests ^main --merges` returns empty (no merge commits introduced on feat).
19. `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` returns empty (WO-0044 regression guard untouched).

20. `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` exits 0 (subject to WO-0068 lessons §verifier note: `models::test_coderankembed_inference` is artefact-gated; run `bash scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` FIRST if not yet run in the verifier session env).

21. `scripts/gate-check.sh 3` may exit 1 due to standing-protocol coverage workaround (sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER interaction) and pre-existing effectiveness-gate flakes; verifier confirms substantive coverage via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` ≥ 80% (line) per scope_in #26 standing protocol.
22. RFR includes the full M1/M2/M3 mutation contract section per scope_in #29.
23. Both feature IDs (P3-W9-F08, P3-W9-F10) are flippable from `passes=false` to `passes=true` by the verifier session.
24. The verifier's measured `ucil-daemon` coverage ≥ 80% line (the WO is purely additive on tests + manifests + scripts; pre-WO baseline preserved or improved per WO-0069 precedent).
acceptance_criteria: test -f plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml && cd /tmp && python3 -c 'import tomllib,sys; m=tomllib.load(open("$REPO/plugins/knowledge/graphiti/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="graphiti"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"' (or equivalent toml-parse + sanity check)
test -f plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/plugin.toml && python3 toml-parse + sanity check (name=codegraphcontext, transport.type=stdio, capabilities.provides[0] starts with 'architecture.')
test -x scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh && bash scripts/devtools/install-graphiti-mcp.sh ; echo $?  # exits 0
cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::graphiti_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'
cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'
git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs  # returns empty (WO-0044 regression guard untouched)
git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs | grep -E '^-' | grep -vE '^---' | wc -l  # returns 0 (existing two tests + mod block top byte-identical; only additive diff)
cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tail -30  # workspace green; coderankembed artefact pre-installed via scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh if not yet run
